title: Tetrastigma hookeri
intro: Tetrastigma hookeri, also known as the Indian vine or Himalayan grape, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region and parts of Southeast Asia, characterized by its woody climber with dark green, ovate leaves and small, white or yellowish flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Tetrastigma hookeri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Tetrastigma hookeri is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or syrup, and is also used in the form of a paste or powder to treat various ailments.
taxonomy: Tetrastigma hookeri is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Vitaceae. It is a type of grapevine native to parts of Asia. The plant is commonly known as the Himalayan grapevine. Tetrastigma hookeri is a climber that grows in the wild in the Himalayan region and other parts of Asia. It is a member of the genus Tetrastigma, which includes several species of grapevines.
